reflectors.name: URF-760

modules.description: Halo-Halo Network DStar DMR YSF by XPR Pinoy

reflectors.description:
URF760

With a Halo Halo Network, it celebrates diversity and connection, mixing voices and cultures like the famous Filipino dessert: colorful, layered, and always refreshing. Add in the XPR Pinoy spirit, and you get a reflector that’s proudly community‑driven, welcoming operators worldwide while keeping a strong Pinoy identity at its core.

In short: URF isn’t just about linking modes—it’s about linking people, networks, and cultures into one vibrant digital space.

reflectors.name: Chandler Hams
reflectors.description:
URF847 is a universal, multi-protocol digital voice reflector that supports D-Star protocols (DPlus, DCS, DExtra and G3), DMR protocols (MMDVMHost, DMR+ and NXDN), M17, P25 (using IMBE) and YSF.
 
DCS847 A
REF847 A
XLX847 A
XRF847 A
 
M17-847
NXDN TG 847
P25 TG 847
TGIF 30847
YSF 00847
 
https://chandlerhams.org/reflector
 
Chandler Ham Radio Club (N7CHN)

reflectors.name: Indiana State Wide All Mode Reflector
reflectors.description:
This is the state wide multi-mode reflector for Indiana. All modes are active but the reflector is still being tested so may be down from time to time.

 

The multi-mode reflector will allow users of one mode to talk with users of that and all other modes at the same time. Uses will include general rag-chewing, state level coordination, and Emergency Management support activities.
